incidents.event: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy seized the Greek-owned container ship Epaminondas in the Strait of Hormuz after firing upon it, causing heavy damage to the navigation bridge. The vessel was escorted to Iranian ports for allegedly operating without proper permits and tampering with navigation systems.

The Panama-flagged container ship MSC Francesca was seized by Iran's IRGC Navy in the Strait of Hormuz for allegedly lacking transit permits and tampering with navigation systems. The vessel was directed to Iranian ports amid escalating tensions.

The container ship Euphoria was fired upon by Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz but escaped damage and continued toward the Gulf of Oman. The vessel was targeted in the same series of incidents as the Epaminondas and MSC Francesca.

The US Navy fired upon and seized the Iranian-flagged container ship Touska in the Gulf of Oman after it allegedly attempted to enter the Strait of Hormuz despite warnings under the US naval blockade.

Approximately 10 vessels in or near the Strait of Hormuz were attacked over a multi-day period, including missile and drone strikes causing casualties and damage. The attacks severely disrupted shipping traffic, reducing tanker traffic by 90%.
Iran's IRGC seized the Marshall Islands-flagged oil tanker Talara in the Strait of Hormuz, citing unauthorized cargo. This was the first confirmed commercial vessel seizure by Iran since mid-2025.
